2100 Greenbrier Lane 
Eureka, California 
95501 
March 23, 1978 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
P.O. Box 807 
Sacramento, California 
95804 
Re: 
Conflict Opinion Request 
Gentlemen: 
I hereby request that you issue an opinion with respect 
to my duties and functions as Assistant Planning Director for 
Humboldt County. 
The facts giving rise to the possible con-
flict are stated below and in the attached letters. 
The County Planning Department wishes to lease a portion 
of an office building in which I own a one-quarter interest. 
I have not participated in any discussions whether or not 
the property should be leased or its terms. 
This matter has 
been handled by other employees in the Planning Department and 
Public Works Department for the County. 
The matters pertaining 
to the lease have been handled by one of my partners, Mr. Allan 
McVicar, who is a local realtor. 
As the letters attached will 
indicate, I opposed the leasing of the property to Humboldt 
County but I was outvoted by my partners. 
Your early opinion with respect to my responsibilities in 
the above mentioned transaction would be appreciated as I have 
no control over whether the lease is executed by the Humboldt 
County Board of Supervisors or by my partners. 
If it is a 
conflict, what do I do and where do I stand? 
Very 
cc: 
Richard Oglesby 
Public Works Department 


2100 Greenbrier Lane 
Eureka, California 
95501 
March 15, 1978 
Carson Block Building 
P.O. Box 122 
Eureka, California 
95501 
Re: 
Leasing of Office Space at Carson Block Building, 3rd 
and F Streets, to County of Humboldt 
Gentlemen: 
This is to advise you that as a County employee and a partner of 
the Carson Block Building, I am not in favor of the pending lease 
between the County of Humboldt and the Carson Block Building. 
My reasons are as follows: 
I was notified by County Counsel of a possible conflict of interest 
on the pending lease. 
They also advised me to seek an opinion 
from the County's District Attorney. 
I went to see Mr. Buffington 
and showed him a copy of the Government Code, Chapter 7 (Conflict 
of Interest) Article 1. 
Mr. Buffington reviewed the code and 
told me he would give me an opinion within a week. 
More than a 
week passed and no word came from Mr. Buffington, so I called him 
and he would not give me a yes or no on the possible conflict. 
He said that he did not think it was a conflict, however, he would 
not advise me to go through with the lease. 
I know that my interest in the building is only 25 per cent and 
you as my partners have the controlling interest and can do what 
you wish, 
b~t I am requesting that you do not go through with the 
pending lease with the County of Humboldt. 
cc: 
Allan McVicar 
Ralph Matsen 
James Turner 
P.S. 
Government Code Section 87100. 
When influencing governmental 
decision prohibited. 
No public official at any level of state or 
local government shall make, participate in making or in any way 
attempt to use his official position to influence a governmental 
decision in which he knows or has reason to know he has a financial 
interest.

As Sam Hinston is one of the four owners of that building,

to Section 83114 uf the GoverllD~nt Code. 
He feels 
this 
section Mr. Winston should write the Fair Political Practices 
CO!lllniS;3ion and ask them for a ruling as 'co 'dhether his int:0:rcsts 
in the proposed building VIould be in conflict v;ith his job as 
a County official. 
If you have further questions, please contact John Cook in 
regard to this matter. 
Until I hear further from you, I will not proceed with.the 
proposed lease. 
cc: 
Stan Mansfield, Planning 
John Ceo k [ County Coun;:;

Mr. Bob London 
CARSON BLOCK 
P. O. BOX 122 
EUREKA, CA 
95501 
Humboldt County Planning Commission 
520 E Street 
Eureka, CA 
95501 
Dear Mr. London: 
~1arch 17, 1978 
You are hereby advised that a meeting of the owners of the Carson 
Block was held on March 17, 1978, at which time three members voted to 
rent to the County of Humboldt and one voted against. 
The person voting against renting to you was Mr. Winston, who has 
1/4 interest in subject property. 
Please understand that any matters pertaining to the office of the 
building, including leasing, etc., is being handled by McVicar Realty and 
Mr. Winston is taking no active interest in the building offices' activities. 
AAMc/cec

sections.question: I hereby request that you issue an opinion with respect to my duties and functions as Assistant Planning Director for Humboldt County. The County Planning Department wishes to lease a portion of an office building in which I own a one-quarter interest. I have not participated in any discussions whether or not the property should be leased or its terms. This matter has been handled by other employees in the Planning Department and Public Works Department for the County. The matters pertaining to the lease have been handled by one of my partners, Mr. Allan McVicar, who is a local realtor. As the letters attached will indicate, I opposed the leasing of the property to Humboldt County but I was outvoted by my partners. Your early opinion with respect to my responsibilities in the above mentioned transaction would be appreciated as I have no control over whether the lease is executed by the Humboldt County Board of Supervisors or by my partners. If it is a conflict, what do I do and where do I stand?
sections.question_synthetic: Does an Assistant Planning Director's one-quarter ownership interest in a building that the County Planning Department seeks to lease create a conflict of interest under Government Code Section 87100, and what are the official's duties and responsibilities in this situation?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter dated March 23, 1978, not an FPPC response. The document includes supporting correspondence from March 1, 15, and 17, 1978 providing context. No FPPC conclusion section exists. The requestor's name appears to be Sam Winston based on referenced communications, though the original request letter is unsigned.

embedding.summary: An Assistant Planning Director for Humboldt County seeks an FPPC opinion regarding whether his 25% ownership interest in a building proposed for lease to the County constitutes a conflict of interest under Government Code Section 87100. The requestor claims no participation in lease discussions and actively opposed the lease, which was approved by the other partners.
